introduction: This spec defines the requirements for the af-hub web UI scaffold — a minimal frontend project at `web/` using Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui that establishes the build toolchain and development pipeline so future specs can add functional pages incrementally.

glossary.@typescript-eslint/recommended: The TypeScript ESLint ruleset extended on top of the Vite-generated default ESLint configuration.
glossary.Go backend: The existing Go server in the af-hub repository that listens on port 8080 and exposes /api, /healthz, and /readyz endpoints.
glossary.React Router: A client-side routing library installed in the frontend project but not yet used in the scaffold phase.
glossary.SPA: Single-Page Application — a client-side rendered web application where navigation is handled in the browser without full page reloads.
glossary.TanStack Query: A library for API state management installed in the frontend project but not yet used in the scaffold phase.
glossary.dev proxy: The Vite development server proxy configuration that forwards matching requests to the Go backend, eliminating CORS issues during development.
glossary.hot reload: A development server feature that automatically updates the browser when source files change without requiring a full page refresh.
glossary.make web-build: The Makefile target that runs a Vite production build outputting to web/dist/.
glossary.make web-dev: The Makefile target that starts the Vite development server with hot reload.
glossary.make web-lint: The Makefile target that runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking, exiting with code 0 only when there are no errors.
glossary.npx shadcn-ui init: The CLI command used to initialize the shadcn/ui toolchain, generating the components.json configuration file.
glossary.package-lock.json: The npm lockfile committed inside web/ that records the exact resolved versions of all installed dependencies for reproducible builds.
glossary.shadcn/ui: A component library whose primitives are copied into the project tree (rather than used as a versioned dependency) and initialized via npx shadcn-ui init.
glossary.web/: The frontend project root directory at the repository root, containing its own package.json and all frontend source files, cleanly separated from the Go backend.
glossary.web/components.json: The shadcn/ui configuration file generated by running npx shadcn-ui init, committed to the repository.
glossary.web/dist/: The output directory produced by a Vite production build, containing the compiled and bundled frontend assets.
glossary.web/index.html: Vite's required HTML entry point file at the frontend project root.
glossary.web/src/App.tsx:
The root React component file that contains the placeholder

af-hub

heading for the scaffold.
glossary.web/src/main.tsx: The application entry point TypeScript/React file that mounts the React app into the DOM.
glossary.web/tsconfig.json: The TypeScript compiler configuration file for the frontend project.

user_story.goal: have a cleanly separated frontend project at web/ with its own package.json
user_story.benefit: I can work on the frontend independently from the Go backend without toolchain conflicts

acceptance_criteria.action: provides a `web/` directory at the repository root containing a standalone frontend project with its own package.json, cleanly separated from the Go backend
uses npm as the package manager and commits `package-lock.json` inside `web/`
includes compiler options: strict=true, moduleResolution=bundler, target=ESNext, and jsx=react-jsx
commits `web/components.json` confirming that `npx shadcn-ui init` was run as part of scaffold setup

edge_cases.action: fails with a clear error indicating the frontend project is not properly initialized at `web/`
edge_cases.return_contract: npm commands run from web/ exit with non-zero code and report missing package.json
edge_cases.error_condition: package.json at web/ is absent or located at the repository root rather than inside web/

user_story.goal: have Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, and React Router configured in the frontend project
user_story.benefit: I can build, develop, and lint the frontend using a standard, reproducible toolchain

acceptance_criteria.action: uses Vite as the build tool and development server
uses React as the UI framework
includes Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
installs TanStack Query for future API state management
installs React Router for future client-side routing
produces a production build outputting compiled assets to `web/dist/` and exits with code 0
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: process exits with code 0; web/dist/ directory is created and contains at least one HTML file and bundled JS/CSS assets
acceptance_criteria.trigger: npm run build (or make web-build) is invoked

acceptance_criteria.action: runs ESLint with the `@typescript-eslint/recommended` ruleset and TypeScript type checking, reporting all errors
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: process exits with code 0 when there are no errors; exits with non-zero code when errors are found
acceptance_criteria.trigger: npm run lint (or make web-lint) is invoked

edge_cases.action: fails the build and reports all type errors
edge_cases.return_contract: npm run build exits with non-zero code and prints TypeScript error diagnostics to stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: TypeScript compilation errors exist in source files

edge_cases.action: reports all violations and exits with non-zero code
edge_cases.return_contract: npm run lint exits with non-zero code and prints violation descriptions including file, line, and rule name
edge_cases.error_condition: ESLint finds rule violations in source files

edge_cases.action: npm run build and npm run lint fail with a descriptive error indicating that npm install must be run first
edge_cases.return_contract: process exits with non-zero code; error message references missing modules

user_story.goal: have the Vite dev server proxy /api, /healthz, and /readyz requests to the Go backend at http://localhost:8080
user_story.benefit: I can develop the frontend without CORS issues or needing CORS middleware on the backend

acceptance_criteria.action: forwards the request to the `Go backend` at http://localhost:8080 preserving the original path
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: the response received from the Go backend is returned verbatim to the requesting browser client
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request matching /api, /healthz, or /readyz is made to the Vite dev server

acceptance_criteria.action: is configured in the Vite configuration such that the proxy behavior is active whenever the Vite dev server runs, without requiring any additional steps from the developer

edge_cases.action: fails the proxied request at the network level with a connection error; no special error handling or retry logic is required for the scaffold phase
edge_cases.return_contract: the browser receives a network-level connection error (e.g., ECONNREFUSED); no custom error page or status code wrapping is required
edge_cases.error_condition: the `Go backend` is not running when a proxied request is made

edge_cases.action: serves the request from the Vite dev server directly without proxying
edge_cases.return_contract: Vite handles the request normally (e.g., serves static assets or the SPA index.html)
edge_cases.error_condition: a request path does not match /api, /healthz, or /readyz

user_story.goal: have ESLint configured with the Vite-generated default extended by @typescript-eslint/recommended
user_story.benefit: I can enforce TypeScript-aware linting rules from the start of the project

acceptance_criteria.action: uses the Vite-generated default ESLint config as the starting point and extends it with the `@typescript-eslint/recommended` ruleset
is expressed in either flat config format (eslint.config.js) or legacy format (.eslintrc.cjs), left to the implementer based on installed toolchain versions

edge_cases.action: ESLint fails to start and reports a missing plugin/config error
edge_cases.return_contract: npm run lint exits with non-zero code and an error message identifying the missing package
edge_cases.error_condition: the `@typescript-eslint/recommended` ruleset package is not installed

user_story.goal: have make web-dev, make web-build, and make web-lint targets in the root Makefile
user_story.benefit: I can operate the frontend build pipeline using the same make-based interface as the Go backend

acceptance_criteria.action: starts the Vite dev server with `hot reload` in the `web/` directory
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: the Vite dev server process starts and remains running, printing its URL to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: runs a Vite production build and outputs compiled assets to `web/dist/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: make exits with code 0 and web/dist/ is populated with bundled assets; exits with non-zero code on build failure

acceptance_criteria.action: runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking via npm run lint in `web/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: make exits with code 0 when there are no errors; exits with non-zero code and prints diagnostics when errors are found

edge_cases.action: the make target fails and prints an actionable error message indicating that npm install must be run in `web/` first
edge_cases.return_contract: make exits with non-zero code; error output directs the developer to run npm install
edge_cases.error_condition: make web-build or make web-lint is invoked but npm dependencies in web/ are not installed

requirements.title: Placeholder route and source file layout

user_story.goal: have a single placeholder route at / that renders an <h1> heading containing 'af-hub'
user_story.benefit: I can verify the scaffold works end-to-end before adding any functional pages

acceptance_criteria.action: provides `web/index.html` as Vite's required HTML entry point
provides `web/src/main.tsx` as the application entry point that mounts the React app into the DOM
contains a root React component that renders an <h1> element with the text 'af-hub' and nothing else
serves the `SPA` and renders an <h1> element containing 'af-hub' in the browser
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: the browser displays a page containing an <h1> element with text content exactly 'af-hub'
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a developer navigates to the Vite dev server URL in a browser

edge_cases.action: Vite build fails with an error identifying the missing entry point
edge_cases.return_contract: npm run build exits with non-zero code and an error message referencing the missing file

edge_cases.action: Vite build fails with an error identifying the missing HTML entry point
edge_cases.return_contract: npm run build exits with non-zero code and an error message referencing the missing index.html

correctness_properties.title: Production build reproducibility
correctness_properties.for_any: state of the web/ source files after npm install using the committed `package-lock.json`
correctness_properties.invariant: npm run build always exits with code 0 and produces output in `web/dist/` containing at least one HTML file

correctness_properties.title: Lint clean baseline
correctness_properties.for_any: source file in web/src/ as shipped by the scaffold
correctness_properties.invariant: npm run lint exits with code 0 and reports zero errors against the `@typescript-eslint/recommended` ruleset

correctness_properties.title: TypeScript strict mode compliance
correctness_properties.for_any: source file in web/src/ as shipped by the scaffold
correctness_properties.invariant: `web/tsconfig.json` with strict=true produces zero type errors when checked against all scaffold source files

correctness_properties.title: Proxy path coverage
correctness_properties.for_any: request path that begins with /api, /healthz, or /readyz
correctness_properties.invariant: the `dev proxy` forwards the request to http://localhost:8080 and does not serve a local asset or SPA fallback instead

correctness_properties.title: Placeholder route renders correct heading
correctness_properties.for_any: browser navigation to the Vite dev server root URL /
correctness_properties.invariant: the rendered DOM contains exactly one <h1> element whose text content is 'af-hub'

execution_paths.title: Developer verifies scaffold end-to-end via dev server

steps.action: runs make web-dev from the repository root
executes npm run dev inside web/, starting the Vite dev server with hot reload
compiles web/src/main.tsx and web/src/App.tsx and begins serving the SPA on its default URL
navigates to the Vite dev server URL in a browser
receives the SPA HTML and JavaScript bundle from the Vite dev server
mounts the React app via web/src/main.tsx and renders web/src/App.tsx, displaying an <h1> element containing 'af-hub'
runs make web-build from the repository root
executes npm run build inside web/
compiles TypeScript, bundles React components, and applies Tailwind CSS, outputting assets to web/dist/
exits with code 0 indicating a successful build
observes web/dist/ populated with HTML, JS, and CSS assets confirming the build succeeded

execution_paths.title: Developer runs lint and type checking

steps.action: runs make web-lint from the repository root
executes npm run lint inside web/
checks all TypeScript source files against the @typescript-eslint/recommended ruleset
performs a type-only check (tsc --noEmit or equivalent) on all source files
exits with code 0 when no linting or type errors are found, or exits with non-zero code and prints diagnostics when errors exist

execution_paths.title: Developer verifies dev proxy by making an API request

steps.action: starts the Go backend (listening on port 8080) and runs make web-dev
starts and applies the dev proxy configuration for /api, /healthz, and /readyz
makes a request to /healthz via the Vite dev server URL in the browser or curl
intercepts the /healthz request and forwards it to the Go backend at http://localhost:8080
handles the request and returns a response
returns the Go backend response verbatim to the requester, confirming the proxy is operational

error_handling.condition: npm run build encounters TypeScript compilation errors
error_handling.behavior: Vite aborts the build, prints type error diagnostics to stderr, and exits with non-zero code; web/dist/ is not produced or is left in a partial state

error_handling.condition: npm run lint finds ESLint rule violations
error_handling.behavior: ESLint prints all violations with file name, line number, and rule name, then exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: node_modules/ is absent when npm run build or npm run lint is invoked
error_handling.behavior: the command fails immediately with an error referencing missing modules, directing the developer to run npm install

error_handling.condition: the Go backend is not running when a proxied request reaches the dev proxy
error_handling.behavior: the dev proxy fails the request with a network-level connection error (ECONNREFUSED); no retry or custom error wrapping is applied
ESLint fails to initialize and reports a missing plugin or config error; npm run lint exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: make web-build or make web-lint invoked without npm dependencies installed in web/
error_handling.behavior: the make target fails and prints an actionable error directing the developer to run npm install inside web/

error_handling.condition: web/src/main.tsx or web/src/App.tsx is absent at build time
error_handling.behavior: Vite build fails with an error identifying the missing entry point file and exits with non-zero code

error_handling.condition: web/index.html is absent at build time
error_handling.behavior: Vite build fails with an error identifying the missing HTML entry point and exits with non-zero code

external_apis.version: unverified — version selected by implementer at install time; locked in package-lock.json

symbols.name: defineConfig
symbols.notes: Used in the Vite configuration file to define build and proxy settings. Exact signature may vary by Vite major version — verify against installed version.

external_apis.version: unverified — current CLI version at time of npx invocation

symbols.name: npx shadcn-ui init
symbols.notes: Initializes shadcn/ui in the project, generating components.json. The exact CLI flags and prompts depend on the installed version. The PRD does not include a Verified External API section — verify the current CLI invocation before implementation.
